Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The ANSI art "telecomics" of the 1992 election 44 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. OpenBSD on Motorola 88000 Processors 100 upvotes, 11 comments
  3. I Built an Open-World Engine for the N64 [video] 403 upvotes, 67 comments
  4. Founder of GitLab battles cancer by founding companies 1094 upvotes, 213 comments
  5. A Verilog to Factorio Compiler and Simulator (Working RISC-V CPU) 75 upvotes, 9 comments
  6. CSS is DOOMed 354 upvotes, 81 comments
  7. Linux is an interpreter 209 upvotes, 51 comments
  8. Cat Itecture: Better Cat Window Boxes (2023) 56 upvotes, 10 comments
  9. Spanish legislation as a Git repo 747 upvotes, 223 comments
  10. The Loneliness of a Room of One's Own 22 upvotes, 2 comments
  11. OpenCiv1 – open-source rewrite of Civ1 150 upvotes, 43 comments
  12. Nonfiction Publishing, Under Threat, Is More Important 14 upvotes, 1 comments
  13. I decompiled the White House's new app 533 upvotes, 194 comments
  14. What if AI doesn't need more RAM but better math? 35 upvotes, 7 comments
  15. Technology: The (nearly) perfect USB cable tester does exist 76 upvotes, 24 comments
  16. The Last Contract: William T. Vollmann's Battle to Publish an Epic (2025) 27 upvotes, 6 comments
  17. A laser-based process that enables adhesive-free paper packaging 82 upvotes, 35 comments
  18. Siclair Microvision (1977) 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  19. South Korea Mandates Solar Panels for Public Parking Lots 305 upvotes, 173 comments
  20. The Many Roots of Our Suffering: Reflections on Robert Trivers (1943–2026) 19 upvotes, 6 comments
  21. Further human + AI + proof assistant work on Knuth's "Claude Cycles" problem 214 upvotes, 137 comments
  22. The Hackers Who Tracked My Sleep Cycle 12 upvotes, 3 comments
  23. Alzheimer's disease mortality among taxi and ambulance drivers (2024) 133 upvotes, 84 comments
  24. AI overly affirms users asking for personal advice 657 upvotes, 504 comments
  25. Lat.md: Agent Lattice: a knowledge graph for your codebase, written in Markdown 7 upvotes, 1 comments
  26. I turned my Kindle into my own personal newspaper 33 upvotes, 17 comments
  27. Show HN: Public transit systems as data – lines, stations, railcars, and history 11 upvotes, 4 comments
  28. Solar is winning the energy race 59 upvotes, 55 comments
  29. Android’s new sideload settings will carry over to new devices 94 upvotes, 135 comments
  30. InpharmD (YC W21) Is Hiring – Senior Ruby on Rails Developer 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 7:00pm, 29 March 2026. Source here.
